statement: L3 can remain the operational default in V3 if a pre-baseline gate shows preserved model separation, stable abstain behavior, and no rubric-swap direction reversals versus L2.
rationale: Because L3 is fixed in most tiers, gate-based evidence is needed before interpreting downstream differences as primarily model-driven.
keyAssumptions: A small gate set is representative enough for pilot-level decisions.
Separation and stability metrics are sensitive to semantic-level confounding.
validationCriteria: A1 l2/l3 checks do not contradict A2/B/Swap directional conclusions.
Abstain-on/off deltas remain interpretable under L3.
critiquePoints: Gate results may still be sample-sensitive in contested concept domains.
questions: Should low-contestation controls be required before locking default-L3 interpretation?
